pageTypes.reason: table list shells frequently drift in header/query-filter/table/pagination ownership when the host boundary is not absorbed by a shared carrier
stat/search/table shells need a stable shared carrier to keep metric, query-filter, table, and pagination sequencing machine-checkable
single-workspace inline-tree tables can remain on the direct standard component path when runtime bridge proof is already available
split workspace shells require governed shared carriers so split ownership and scroll boundaries do not drift into business pages
drawer shell ownership, header rhythm, and footer docking should be frozen at the project carrier layer before business slot fill
detail drawer pages can keep using the direct standard component path while project carriers are still optional
feedback pages have low skeleton variance and can remain on the runtime-bridged standard component path
header/footer/action ownership on full-page-edit should be stabilized through a shared project carrier before rollout
detail shells can stay on the direct standard component path when no project-specific host carrier is required

notes: This file only applies to legacy-host-compatible delivery policy planning.
It does not create a parallel generation family; page-component remains the normalized primary chain.
Projects may still override or extend these defaults through project facts, but planner decisions must remain policy-driven.
